homepage.text_excerpt: --> Ruth Cadbury MP | Labour MP | Brentford & Isleworth Constituency top of page About All news Advice More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Stay Updated First name * Last name * Email * You can opt out at any time. Ruth's privacy policy is here . Subscribe Your local voice in Parliament I am honoured to have served as your MP since 2015, working hard for this community, listening to what matters to you and representing your views in Parliament. ​I have lived here in the constituency for over 30 years. That is why I am passionate about being a champion for our community in Parliament and beyond. ​ ​Since first being elected, I have responded to requests for help and support at the rate of 10,000 per year. Each month I visit schools, community groups, residents' associations and businesses. ​ I will always work to remove the barriers to opportunity - fighting to make our part of West London fairer, greener and more prosperous. ​ I currently sit as Chair of the Transport Select Committee , whose job it is to scrutinise the transport industry and transport policy. I have held a number of shadow ministerial briefs , including; Prisons, International Trade, and housing and local government. News In the Chamber... Westminster Insight Day I am running a Westminster Insight Day to bring local young people to Parliament to learn more about our politics and democracy. I previously ran one over the February half term and received overwhelmingly positive feedback - 100% of attendees said that they would recommend the Insight Day to a friend. The day will give young people an opportunity to learn about Parliament as well as contribute to my work as an MP and gain first-hand experience of Westminster politics. Please Ruth calls for a ban on goods entering the UK from illegal Israeli Settlements Ruth speaks out about the infected blood scandal For a full list of my spoken contributions, visit Spoken contributions of Ruth Cadbury - MPs and Lords - UK Parliament View all articles Constituency update... The day will give young people an opportunity to learn about Parliament as well as contribute to my work as an MP and gain first-hand experience of Westminster politics. Please Ruth welcomes new £30 million funding for youth services Young people in the Boroughs of Hounslow and Richmond are set to benefit from a major new £30 million investment in youth services, as the Mayor of London, Sadiq Khan, launches the landmark ‘Youth Lates’ programme to bring high-quality youth clubs back into every community. The programme will deliver at least one late-night youth club in every London borough, giving young people safe spaces to go after school, in the evenings and at weekends — with access to activities, mento Newsletter - April 2026 International Women’s Day enabled us to celebrate and be grateful for the contribution to local and to national life of so many amazing women. With Seema Malhotra MP, I hosted an event in Hounslow where we recognised the achievements of around 40 local Women. And, in Westminster, the Speaker hosted the annual 100 Women in Westminster celebration. pages.title: Westminster Insight Day
headings: h2: News Advice and support Contact
pages.text_excerpt: --> Westminster Insight Day top of page About All news Advice More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. All Posts Newsletter News Search Westminster Insight Day Apr 21 1 min read I am running a Westminster Insight Day to bring local young people to Parliament to learn more about our politics and democracy. I previously ran one over the February half term and received overwhelmingly positive feedback - 100% of attendees said that they would recommend the Insight Day to a friend. The day will give young people an opportunity to learn about Parliament as well as contribute to my work as an MP and gain first-hand experience of Westminster politics. Please note, the Insight Day is only available to young people aged 16-19 who either live or go to school in my constituency (you can check with your postcode here: Find your MP - MPs and Lords - UK Parliament ). We have limited spaces due to capacity and will be operating on a first-come first-served basis - but do not worry as I will plan to hold other events later in the year. If you would like to be prioritised for future events, please email ruth.cadbury.mp@parliament.uk Details: 28th May 2026, 9am - 5pm House of Commons, SW1A 0AA Sign up here (the form will close once full or on 5th May): https://caroline3853.wixforms.com/f/7443255606430926777 Tags: Constituency Parliament Recent Posts See All Newsletter - April 2026 Tribute to Cllr Jagdish Sharma Newsletter - March 2026 Home News Advice and support Contact © Ruth Cadbury MP 2025 Privacy Policy Useful Information Useful Contacts Immigration Universal Credit Behaviour Policy Archive Archive News Archive Video News Archive Newsletters bottom of page
